name: find scrolls the RENDERED preview to a match (auto-preview path)
comment: Regression guard for the find-scroll bug: Find found matches (the count incremented) but never scrolled the preview WebView, so a match below the fold stayed off screen — because selectMatch scrolled the hidden editor's NSTextView, not the preview. FIXED by scrolling the preview to the match's source line when the preview is showing. This plan replaces the old KNOWN-FAILING version, which encoded the bug as acceptable (expected red) and entered via --no-auto-preview + View menu — the door users never take. It now enters via AUTO-PREVIEW and asserts the preview actually moved, using the --expose-scroll-fraction test hook (AX can't read a WKWebView's scroll offset). NEGATIVE CONTROL: assert the preview is at the top before searching, so 'it scrolled' can't be a false pass. Requires staged /tmp/medit-ap-long.md and the Debug build launched with --expose-scroll-fraction.

steps.comment: Door check: preview web view present (auto-preview engaged). A hidden NSTextView still exposes its AX element, so assert the preview IS up rather than the editor absent.
NEGATIVE CONTROL: preview begins at the top, so a post-find non-zero fraction proves the FIND caused the scroll.
'Paragraph 120' is the last paragraph of long.md — a match near the very bottom, so a working scroll produces a large fraction.
Commit the search / go to the match.
THE ASSERTION: the preview scrolled toward the bottom to reveal the match. A regression (scrolling the hidden editor) leaves this at 0.000.
